title: concatenate array and queue causes C++ compile error
body:
This code produces a C++ compile error:
```
module top;
    byte foo[$] = {'hab};
    byte bar[2] = {'hf0, 'h0d};
    initial begin
        foo = {foo, bar};
        $display("foo: %p", foo);
    end
endmodule
```
The error is:
```
In file included from Vqueue_cpp_error__ALL.cpp:8:
Vqueue_cpp_error___024root__DepSet_h0b9516b8__0__Slow.cpp: In function ‘void Vqueue_cpp_error___024root___eval_initial__TOP(Vqueue_cpp_error___024root*)’:
Vqueue_cpp_error___024root__DepSet_h0b9516b8__0__Slow.cpp:46:71: error: cannot convert ‘VlUnpacked<unsigned char, 2>’ to ‘const VlQueue<unsigned char>&’
   46 |     vlSelfRef.top__DOT__foo = VlQueue<CData/*7:0*/>::consCC(vlSelfRef.top__DOT__bar,
      |                                                             ~~~~~~~~~~^~~~~~~~~~~~~
      |                                                                       |
      |                                                                       VlUnpacked<unsigned char, 2>
In file included from /tools/rhel9/msd/msd_dvs-v3.0.0.testing/share/verilator/include/verilated.h:1032,
                 from Vqueue_cpp_error__pch.h:21,
                 from Vqueue_cpp_error.cpp:4,
                 from Vqueue_cpp_error__ALL.cpp:3:
/tools/rhel9/msd/msd_dvs-v3.0.0.testing/share/verilator/include/verilated_types.h:549:42: note:   initializing argument 1 of ‘static VlQueue<T_Value, N_MaxSize> VlQueue<T_Value, N_MaxSize>::consCC(const VlQueue<T_Value, N_MaxSize>&, const VlQueue<T_Value, N_MaxSize>&) [with T_Value = unsigned char; long unsigned int N_MaxSize = 0]’
  549 |     static VlQueue consCC(const VlQueue& lhs, const VlQueue& rhs) {
      |                           ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~^~~
make: *** [/tools/rhel9/msd/msd_dvs-v3.0.0.testing/share/verilator/include/verilated.mk:272: Vqueue_cpp_error__ALL.o] Error 1
rm Vqueue_cpp_error__ALL.verilator_deplist.tmp
make: Leaving directory '/home/bmurdock/work/examples/systemverilog/obj_dir'
%Error: make -C obj_dir -f Vqueue_cpp_error.mk -j 32 exited with 2
```
In the commercial simulator I tried this code compiles and runs fine, but looking at the SystemVerilog spec I can't decide for myself if it should or not.  I'm guessing that even if it shouldn't be allowed, this isn't the way verilator should fail.

What 'verilator' command line do we use to run your example?
```
verilator -j $(nproc) -o foo --binary queue_cpp_error.sv && obj_dir/foo
```
What 'verilator --version' are you using?  
```
 $ verilator --version
Verilator 5.039 devel rev v5.038-103-g0da9f6eb0
```
Did you try it with the git master version?

Yes, that is the latest git master

What OS and distribution are you using?

Tried on archlinux and Rocky Linux 9

May we assist you in trying to fix this in Verilator yourself?

Sure!
